content.problemStatement:
In a country **Stone Land**, there are NNN stones arranged in a row from left to right. Each stone has some height represented by a positive number. The stones are numbered from 111 to NNN from left to right.

You are given an array **H** of size NNN consisting of the height of the NNN stones from left to
right.

Stone at jthj^{th}jth position is visible from the stone at ithi^{th}ith position if:-


j>ij>ij>i
max(H[i],H[i+1],H[i+2],....,H[j−1])max(H[i],H[i+1],H[i+2],....,H[j-1])max(H[i],H[i+1],H[i+2],....,H[j−1]) <<< H[j]H[j]H[j]
You are asked MMM queries.


Each query consists of two different positions of stones AAA and BBB.
You have to Print the total number of stones that are visible from both the stones at the position AAA and BBB.
constraints: 1≤H[i]≤1091 \leq H[i] \leq 10^91≤H[i]≤109, where **H[i]** denotes the height of ithi^{th}ith stone, and 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N
It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.
It is guaranteed that the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM, where NNN is the number of stones and MMM is the number of queries.
The Second line consists of NNN space-separated integers **(array H)** denoting the height of stones from left to the right separated by a space.
Next, MMM lines consist of two space-separated integers AAA and BBB separated by space where AAA and BBB are positions of stones.
content.outputFormat:
For each query, print the total number of stones that are visible from both the stones at the position AAA and BBB in a new line.
Example Input`1
6 6
3 10 20 20 5 50
1 2
2 4
4 3
4 5
1 3
4 4
`Example Output`2
1
1
1
1
1
`Explanation**Query 111**: Stone at position 111 can see the stones at position (2,3,6)(2, 3, 6)(2,3,6) whereas stone at position 222 can see the stones at position (3,6)(3, 6)(3,6). Thus, the total number of stones that are visible from both stones is 222.
